chunks.text: The Black Engineering and Science Student Association (BESSA), and Black Graduate Engineering and Science Students (BGESS), celebrated their 55th and 35th anniversaries, respectively. The anniversary celebration and community building event, which took place on February 11, 2023, was hosted by the Black Engineering and Science Alumni Club (BESAC).
chunks.question: What anniversaries did BESSA and BGESS celebrate?

chunks.text: We invite you to join the EECS department in recognizing a previously overlooked hero, Berkeley EE Prof. Joseph T. Gier, the University of California’s first tenured Black professor. Raised in Oakland by a single mother, Gier came to Berkeley.
chunks.question: Who was Joseph T. Gier?

chunks.text: EECS Assistant Prof. Sophia Shao, Associate Prof. Prabal Dutta, and alumnus Deepak Pathak have won 2022 Okawa Foundation Research Grants. The Okawa Foundation for Information and Telecommunications recognizes studies and analyses in the fields of information and telecommunications.
chunks.question: Who won 2022 Okawa Foundation Research Grants?
chunks.answer: Sophia Shao, Prabal Dutta, Deepak Pathak.

chunks.text: EECS Associate Prof. Prabal Dutta has won the 2022 ACM SenSys Test of Time Award. The paper by Dutta, Dawson-Haggerty (Ph.D. ‘14), Chen, Liang, and Terzis titled, 'Design and Evaluation of a Versatile and Efficient Receiver-Initiated Link Layer for Low-Power Wireless,' was recognized for pioneering the use of synchronous transmissions.
chunks.question: What award did Prabal Dutta win?
chunks.answer: 2022 ACM SenSys Test of Time Award.

chunks.text: EECS Prof. Emeritus and alumnus David A. Hodges (M.S. 1961; Ph.D. 1966) passed away on November 13th. He was 85. A former engineering dean and EECS department chair, Prof. Hodges began his career at Bell Telephone Laboratories before joining the EECS faculty in 1970.
chunks.question: Who passed away on November 13th?
chunks.answer: EECS Prof. Emeritus David A. Hodges.

chunks.text: EE alumna and EECS Prof. Emerita Constance Chang-Hasnain (Ph.D. ’87) has won the 2022 Welker Award at Compound Semiconductor Week (CSW). She was cited for pioneering contributions to VCSEL photonics, nano-photonics and high contrast metastructures for optical communications and optical sensing.
chunks.question: What award did Constance Chang-Hasnain win?

chunks.text: EECS alumnus Paul Debevec (Ph.D. ‘96, advisor: Jitendra Malik) will receive the Charles F. Jenkins Lifetime Achievement Award at the Television Academy’s 74th Engineering, Science & Technology Emmy Awards. The award recognizes Debevec for his pioneering work on high dynamic range imaging, image-based lighting, and photogrammetry.
chunks.question: What award will Paul Debevec receive?
chunks.answer: Charles F. Jenkins Lifetime Achievement Award.

chunks.text: EECS alumnus Gary S. May (M.S. ’88/Ph.D. ’91, advisor: Costas Spanos), the first Black chancellor of UC Davis, is the focus of an Inside Higher Ed story highlighting the unique bond between three Black Berkeley Engineering alumni in the 1980s.
chunks.question: Who is Gary S. May?
chunks.answer: First Black chancellor of UC Davis.

chunks.text: EECS alumna Leslie Field (M.S. ’88/Ph.D. 91, advisor: Richard White), the first woman to earn a doctorate from the Berkeley Sensor & Actuator Center (BSAC), has won the 2022 Mark Shannon Grand Challenges Award. This award recognizes long-term contributions of members of our technical community with a vision to address humanity’s challenges.
chunks.question: What award did Leslie Field win?
